paper_title: Visually Informed Binaural Audio Generation without Binaural Audios

problem: Lack of a high-quality, linguistically rich Bahasa dataset limits the development and evaluation of TTS systems.
method: Curate and release a 55-hour, 52K-utterance Bahasa corpus recorded by balanced male/female professional speakers in a controlled studio, sourced from diverse text (Wikipedia + GPT-translated content), with standardized splits and detailed statistics.
paper_title: Bahasa Harmony: A Comprehensive Dataset for Bahasa Text-to-Speech Synthesis with Discrete Codec Modeling of EnGen-TTS.

problem: Generating natural, speaker-consistent Bahasa speech efficiently while bridging phonetic–text misalignment in Latin script TTS.
method: EnGen-TTS: a neural codec language model that autoregressively predicts EnCodec tokens conditioned via dual cross-attention on frozen mT5 text embeddings and speaker embeddings (cross-attn instead of concatenation), using only the first RVQ codebook for phonetic/content tokens; combined with a HiFi-GAN vocoder and a composite loss (cross-entropy on tokens + mel + GAN) to achieve high MOS (4.45±0.13) with near real-time RTF.
paper_title: Bahasa Harmony: A Comprehensive Dataset for Bahasa Text-to-Speech Synthesis with Discrete Codec Modeling of EnGen-TTS.

problem: TTS models often require language-specific fine-tuning and struggle to generalize across languages and speakers.
method: Pretrain the codec LM on LJ-Speech and VCTK with a multilingual mT5 text encoder, enabling strong cross-lingual transfer (zero/few-shot) and robust multilingual performance; demonstrate consistent MOS gains across 7 languages, including Bahasa, without sacrificing efficiency (KV-cache at inference).
paper_title: Bahasa Harmony: A Comprehensive Dataset for Bahasa Text-to-Speech Synthesis with Discrete Codec Modeling of EnGen-TTS.

problem: ASR error detection models typically rely only on transcript text and fail to effectively exploit ASR-internal signals, limiting accuracy in flagging word-level transcription errors, especially under varying audio conditions.
method: RED-ACE: a confidence-aware BERT sequence tagger that jointly encodes transcript tokens and ASR word-level confidence scores by quantizing confidences into bins and adding a learned confidence embedding layer summed with token/segment/position embeddings; trained end-to-end for ERROR/NOTERROR tagging, outperforming thresholding, heuristic fusion, and score concatenation baselines and yielding robust gains across recording qualities and different ASR models.
paper_title: RED-ACE: Robust Error Detection for ASR using Confidence Embeddings

problem: Progress on AED is hindered by the lack of a public benchmark containing ASR hypotheses, word-level confidences, and aligned error labels, with cloud ASR changes undermining reproducibility.
method: Released a new AED dataset built from LibriSpeech by decoding with Google Cloud Speech-to-Text (default and video models), capturing word-level confidence scores and labeling ERROR/NOTERROR via minimum-edit alignment to references, with clean/other splits enabling main and cross-quality robustness evaluation.
paper_title: RED-ACE: Robust Error Detection for ASR using Confidence Embeddings

problem: Context-aware ASR struggles with rare/OOV terms, and existing contextual biasing often needs fine-tuning, structured catalogs, or access to model internals—unsuitable for black-box ASR.
method: CB-RAG: a model-agnostic, embedding-based context constructor. It encodes a large static vocabulary (with definitions) using MiniLM (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), forms a query from the concatenated transcripts of the previous k VAD segments, retrieves top-N nearest words via FAISS cosine similarity, and supplies them as the context list to a contextual-biasing ASR—no ASR modification or fine-tuning required, yielding up to 17% relative WER reduction with near-baseline latency.
paper_title: Retrieval Augmented Generation based context discovery for ASR

problem: Lack of a standardized, plug-and-play evaluation of automatic context construction methods for ASR in realistic, black-box settings.
method: An end-to-end, modular pipeline that segments audio via VAD, builds context either by CB-RAG or via LLM prompting from prior transcript windows, and optionally applies post-ASR LLM transcript correction; evaluated on TEDLIUMv3, Earnings21, and SPGISpeech with WER, context overlap, count, and latency metrics—showing CB-RAG offers the best accuracy–latency trade-off compared to LLM-based generation and correction.
paper_title: Retrieval Augmented Generation based context discovery for ASR

problem: Generate text-aligned speech that matches a person’s vocal identity and facial emotion using only visual cues; prior face-to-speech lacks emotional expressiveness and disentanglement without speech prompts.
method: DEmoFace: an RVQ-based discrete diffusion transformer with Multimodal DiT blocks. Identity is extracted from a composite face encoder (ArcFace+FaceNet) and aligned to speech GE2E during training; emotion is obtained via PosterV2 with label-driven, identity-agnostic embeddings. Conditions are injected via AdaLN, while text (SpeechT5) is fused through cross-attention for dynamic text–speech alignment. Discrete diffusion over codec tokens enables parallel, diverse, and natural generation.
paper_title: Emotional Face-to-Speech

problem: Training all hierarchical audio details at once in discrete token space is inefficient and harms intelligibility and expressiveness; RVQ tokens carry frequency-hierarchical information that is underutilized.
method: Coarse-to-fine curriculum learning over RVQ levels: begin with low-frequency (lower-level) tokens and progressively add higher-level tokens, with per-level concrete-score heads in the diffusion model. This exploits RVQ’s frequency hierarchy to improve efficiency, WER, emotion similarity, and speaker consistency.
paper_title: Emotional Face-to-Speech

problem: Standard predictor-free guidance does not extend well to multi-conditional generation (identity, emotion, text) in discrete diffusion, causing entangled attributes and weak global control.
method: Enhanced predictor-free guidance (EPFG): an EBM-inspired formulation that multiplies joint and compositional conditional scores, enabling separate guidance scales per condition plus a joint scale. This yields stronger global consistency and better disentanglement for multi-conditional sampling.
paper_title: Emotional Face-to-Speech

problem: In federated learning, updates from clients with irrelevant or corrupted data destabilize and degrade the global model, and the server lacks visibility into client data to filter them.
method: Model client updates as players in a cooperative game and use the Shapley value (computed via Monte Carlo over permutations) with the server’s validation performance as the characteristic function to estimate each client’s contribution. Accumulate these into a relevance vector and sample clients via a softmax over relevance (S-FedAvg), giving higher selection probability to consistently helpful (and penalizing harmful) clients.
paper_title: Game of Gradients: Mitigating Irrelevant Clients in Federated Learning

problem: The server needs to identify which clients have high-quality data for a specific target class (e.g., to handle class imbalance or targeted incentives).
method: Compute class-specific relevance by restricting the characteristic function’s validation set to only the target class (V_c) and re-running the Shapley computation to obtain per-class relevance scores (ϕ_c), thereby ranking clients that best improve performance on that class.
paper_title: Game of Gradients: Mitigating Irrelevant Clients in Federated Learning

problem: Clients may have permuted/mislabeled local labels (label standardization issue), leading to harmful updates despite owning relevant data.
method: S-FedAvg-Label-Std: after the global model stabilizes, the server sends class-wise precisions; clients partition data by local label, run the global model to find the majority predicted label per partition, and if its fraction exceeds the server’s precision for that label, swap the local label accordingly. The server then resets the client’s relevance toward the mean to re-enable participation after correction.
paper_title: Game of Gradients: Mitigating Irrelevant Clients in Federated Learning

problem: Reliable slip detection during robotic grasp-and-lift is hard across diverse objects and grasp conditions; prior GelSight-based, thresholded heuristics are brittle and miss subtle/early slip.
method: A multimodal, sequence-based DNN that fuses GelSight tactile images and an external RGB camera: per-frame features are extracted with a fixed pretrained CNN (e.g., Inception-V3/VGG), concatenated across modalities, and fed to an LSTM over 8 early-lift frames to classify slip vs. stable grasp, achieving 88.03% accuracy on unseen objects and outperforming tactile-only and prior threshold methods.
paper_title: Slip Detection with Combined Tactile and Visual Information

problem: Vision-only models pretrained on ImageNet underutilize positional/motion cues, yielding poor slip detection from raw frames.
method: Introduce reference-anchored image-difference inputs (128 + I(t) − I(−2)) for both modalities to explicitly encode motion while remaining CNN-friendly, substantially boosting vision-only performance (~55% to ~78–81%) and strengthening tactile–vision fusion.
paper_title: Slip Detection with Combined Tactile and Visual Information

problem: Lack of standardized multimodal data limits learning-based slip detection and assessment of generalization.
method: Collect and release a synchronized tactile–vision dataset from robot grasp-and-lift trials (≈1200 grasps over 94 objects, augmented to 5510 sequences), training on 84 objects and demonstrating generalization to 10 unseen objects with varied shape, material, and weight.
paper_title: Slip Detection with Combined Tactile and Visual Information

problem: Policies trained in simulation often fail to transfer safely or adapt reliably to real robots due to unknown/variable dynamics; domain-randomized policies can be brittle and produce unstable updates during real-world adaptation.
method: Use gradient-based meta-reinforcement learning to learn a policy initialization that rapidly and stably adapts to new dynamics in a few trials. A MAML-style procedure meta-trains over randomized dynamics and backpropagates through inner-loop PPO updates, yielding predictable sim-to-real adaptation demonstrated on a KUKA puck-hitting task.
paper_title: Meta Reinforcement Learning for Sim-to-real Domain Adaptation

problem: Real-world adaptation is sample-inefficient and risky when exploring high-dimensional, unconstrained action spaces.
method: Constrain exploration via a task-specific latent action space by training a VAE trajectory generator on safe robot splines. The policy outputs low-dimensional latent codes (conditioned on goals) that the decoder maps to smooth motor trajectories, enabling safe, low-dimensional exploration and faster adaptation; the learned latents disentangle hitting angle and strength.
paper_title: Meta Reinforcement Learning for Sim-to-real Domain Adaptation

problem: RL with sparse rewards learns slowly due to poor exploration, and dense reward shaping or continuous human-in-the-loop feedback is costly.
method: Brain-Guided RL: train an observation ErrP EEG classifier (modified EEGNet) offline; use its online decoded evaluative signals to supervise learning of a Human-Feedback policy F(s,a) that predicts action optimality; during RL (e.g., PPO) use a decaying mixture of the HF policy and the RL policy as the behavior policy to guide exploration, enabling efficient learning from sparse rewards with limited human involvement.
paper_title: Accelerated Robot Learning via Human Brain Signals

problem: EEG-based evaluative feedback is noisy, low-SNR, and limited in quantity, making it hard to learn reliable guidance in larger state spaces.
method: Robust HF policy learning with design choices to combat noise and label scarcity: discretized low-dimensional action/state interface, a lightweight FC network (1x16) trained with cross-entropy, and a prioritized feedback replay buffer to aggressively reuse recent labels. Validate and tune with a simulated noisy oracle across accuracies to identify regimes (≈60%+) where feedback accelerates RL and to set the HF-mixing schedule.
paper_title: Accelerated Robot Learning via Human Brain Signals

problem: Collecting ground-truth labels for training an EEG ErrP detector on the target robot task is expensive; transfer across tasks is needed.
method: Two-stage calibration-transfer: collect EEG while observing a simpler navigation task where optimal actions are auto-labeled via Dijkstra; train the ErrP detector offline, then apply it to provide online noisy labels on the target task to learn F. This decouples calibration from deployment and reduces expert labeling cost.
paper_title: Accelerated Robot Learning via Human Brain Signals

problem: Uniform grasp candidate sampling treats all scene points equally, wasting computation and lowering accuracy because graspable regions are sparse in cluttered 6-DoF grasping.
method: Define graspness, a geometry-driven graspability measure at point and view levels, computed by a look-ahead search over discretized grasps with an analytic force metric and collision-aware scene projection/normalization to yield graspable landscapes that highlight graspable areas.
paper_title: Graspness Discovery in Clutters for Fast and Accurate Grasp Detection

problem: Exhaustive look-ahead search is too slow for online use; a fast, transferable selector of graspable points/views is needed.
method: Cascaded Graspness Model (CGM): a sparse-conv ResUNet backbone predicts point-wise graspness and objectness; graspable farthest point sampling selects seed points; an MLP predicts view-wise graspness with probabilistic view selection. This plug-and-play module accelerates and boosts accuracy of existing grasp detectors.
paper_title: Graspness Discovery in Clutters for Fast and Accurate Grasp Detection

problem: End-to-end 6-DoF grasp detectors lack early filtering and are inefficient on dense point clouds in cluttered scenes.
method: GSNet, a two-stage network that integrates CGM for early pruning and a Grasp Operation Model that cylinder-groups seed–view neighborhoods and predicts grasp scores/widths over angle–depth grids using shared PointNet features, achieving large AP gains and fast inference on GraspNet-1Billion and strong real-robot success rates.
paper_title: Graspness Discovery in Clutters for Fast and Accurate Grasp Detection

problem: End-to-end robot policies are highly sensitive to the chosen action space (joint vs. Cartesian), and explicit policies either commit to one space or require multi-loss balancing with conflicting gradients.
method: Implicit Kinematic Policies (IKP): an implicit behavior cloning energy model that ingests the same action in multiple parameterizations by sampling in joint space and passing both joint actions and their forward-kinematics-derived Cartesian poses as inputs via a differentiable FK module, allowing the policy to learn inductive patterns across kinematically consistent spaces without loss balancing.
paper_title: Implicit Kinematic Policies: Unifying Joint and Cartesian Action Spaces in End-to-End Robot Learning

problem: Whole-body manipulation benefits from low-frequency, structured trajectories that may appear in different links/spaces, but standard formulations do not expose per-link Cartesian structure to the policy.
method: Autoregressive implicit control that samples each joint dimension sequentially and, at every step, concatenates FK-derived 6-DoF Cartesian poses for each link with previously chosen joints into per-dimension EBMs, trained/inferred via derivative-free sampling; this exposes link-wise Cartesian and joint structure to the model to discover the most learnable representation.
paper_title: Implicit Kinematic Policies: Unifying Joint and Cartesian Action Spaces in End-to-End Robot Learning

problem: Miscalibrated or biased joint encoders induce nonlinear errors in Cartesian space, degrading precision tasks and robustness of policies trained in task space.
method: Residual Forward Kinematics: insert learnable residual blocks around the FK within IKP to model joint-space offsets (linear in joints but nonlinear in Cartesian), leveraging FK’s differentiability to backpropagate and automatically calibrate encoder biases, yielding strong performance under miscalibration.
paper_title: Implicit Kinematic Policies: Unifying Joint and Cartesian Action Spaces in End-to-End Robot Learning

problem: Robots must fuse asynchronous, multi-rate sensory streams (e.g., images, proprioception, force-torque) and act nearly continuously, but standard discrete-time MDP batching loses temporal structure and handles irregular sampling poorly.
method: InFuser: a continuous-time policy using Neural Controlled Differential Equations that conditions on a low-frequency image embedding and is driven by higher-frequency sensor streams to evolve a single multisensory latent state, which is decoded into continuous-time actions—jointly unifying sensor fusion and latent dynamics modeling for end-to-end visuomotor control.
paper_title: Multiscale Sensor Fusion and Continuous Control with Neural CDEs

problem: Neural CDEs typically require smooth interpolants that can introduce noncausal dependencies, conflicting with real-time robotic control and asynchronous sensing.
method: A discretely-online CDE integration scheme that constructs per-interval cubic splines only from past measurements to maintain causality at the control rate (with optional zero-order-hold for fully continuous-time), yielding piecewise-C1 control functions that react immediately to multi-frequency inputs.
paper_title: Multiscale Sensor Fusion and Continuous Control with Neural CDEs

problem: Learning robust visuomotor policies from demonstrations when images are sparse/throttled or dropped, especially for dynamic tasks where temporal abstraction is critical.
method: Behavior cloning with a function-level loss (time integral) to train the CDE policy over control trajectories, showing superior robustness and performance versus stateless fusion and deep DMP baselines on dynamic Ball-in-Cup variants under image throttling and drop regimes, while remaining competitive on quasi-static cloth manipulation.
paper_title: Multiscale Sensor Fusion and Continuous Control with Neural CDEs

problem: Learning strategic pursuit-evasion behavior on real robots is intractable under partial observability because the robot must act under physical state uncertainty and unknown opponent intent using only onboard sensing.
method: Transform the Dec-POMDP into a supervised privileged-learning setup: train a fully-observable teacher policy with access to true relative state and the evader’s future N-step trajectory, encode this into a learned latent intent, and optimize the teacher with PPO; then distill into a student that only receives RGB-D detections filtered by a simple Kalman Filter plus action/state history, using DAGGER to supervise both actions and latent intent. The key novelty is using future-trajectory privileged information to learn predictive latent intent that yields information gathering and anticipatory interception.
paper_title: Learning Vision-based Pursuit-Evasion Robot Policies

problem: Privileged supervision quality for the partially-observable student is sensitive to how the evader is modeled; policies derived under strong game-theoretic assumptions often fail to transfer to real, noisy settings.
method: Systematically vary the teacher’s training opponent (random, MARL, game-theoretic) and show that a balance of behavioral diversity and near-optimality produces better supervision and distillation. Demonstrate rapid OOD adaptation by finetuning only the student’s intent encoder using supervision from the teacher’s latent codes, establishing that coverage (diverse evaders) beats specialization (Nash-optimal) for transfer.
paper_title: Learning Vision-based Pursuit-Evasion Robot Policies

problem: Deploying vision-only pursuit-evasion on a physical quadruped with minimal perception requires handling noisy detections and limited FOV while still acting strategically.
method: Use an off-the-shelf 3D object detector and an unoptimized Kalman Filter to produce relative-state estimates and covariances that the distilled policy exploits to trigger information-gathering (look/scan) and predictive interception. Achieve zero-shot real-world performance against humans and another quadruped using only onboard RGB-D and high-level velocity control.
paper_title: Learning Vision-based Pursuit-Evasion Robot Policies

problem: Real-time on-robot RL for legged locomotion is constrained by compute and sample efficiency, making prior approaches slow, GPU-dependent, and limited in gait quality.
method: An on-robot learning framework built on CrossQ (SAC-based off-policy RL with joint BN over (o,a) and (o',a') and no target networks), keeping UTD=1 to cut compute while improving sample efficiency and stability; enables 40 Hz control and learning robust gaits on a laptop without a GPU in ~8 minutes, outperforming SAC/DroQ in speed, falls, and smoothness.
paper_title: Gait in Eight: Efficient On-Robot Learning for Omnidirectional Quadruped Locomotion

problem: On-robot learning has mostly handled only forward gait tracking and struggles with noisy, partial state estimates, hindering practical omnidirectional locomotion.
method: Task and reward design for omnidirectional velocity tracking in the xy-plane using a rotated velocity error term; relies solely on proprioception with a Kalman-fused trunk velocity estimator (IMU accelerations + leg-odometry) to handle sensor noise and drift, enabling on-robot learning of omnidirectional policies indoors and outdoors.
paper_title: Gait in Eight: Efficient On-Robot Learning for Omnidirectional Quadruped Locomotion

problem: Locomotion controllers must trade off agility/high speed against stability/natural foot clearance, and existing single-architecture approaches underperform on one side of this trade-off.
method: Two control architectures paired with CrossQ: (1) Joint Target Prediction (constrained joint-angle offsets around a nominal stance plus One-Euro filtering and PD tracking) for agile, high-speed gaits; (2) CPG-based control (fixed trot foot-height trajectories with RL-predicted per-foot Cartesian offsets, phase as input, IK + PD tracking) for stable, natural, omnidirectional gaits; empirically show JTP reaches higher speeds while CPG yields more stable learning and better yaw control.
paper_title: Gait in Eight: Efficient On-Robot Learning for Omnidirectional Quadruped Locomotion
